title: Mets vs. Giants Recap: April 2, 2026 Game Results, Stats, and Analysis on CBS Sports
body:
The San Francisco Giants made a resounding statement in their home opener on Thursday night, cruising to a 7-2 victory over the New York Mets behind a standout performance from rookie catcher Daniel Susac. Susac, making his first major league start, proved to be an instant impact player, notching three hits and drawing a walk in a dominant display. The Giants' win was a testament to their depth and talent, as they looked every bit the part of a team primed to make a deep playoff run.

Susac's impressive debut was the perfect catalyst for the Giants' offense, which lit up the scoreboard with a series of big hits. The rookie catcher's ability to reach base and drive in runs set the tone for the rest of the team, and his contributions were just the beginning of what proved to be a balanced attack. The Giants' lineup was a veritable who's who of talent, with players like J.D. Davis and Thairo Estrada making key contributions at the plate. As a result, the Mets struggled to keep pace, ultimately succumbing to a Giants team that seemed to be firing on all cylinders.

One of the key factors in the Giants' success was their ability to get the better of the Mets' pitching staff. San Francisco's hitters were aggressive and selective, working the count and capitalizing on mistakes to the tune of seven runs on 11 hits. In contrast, the Mets struggled to generate any real momentum, managing just two runs on eight hits. The Giants' bullpen was nearly flawless, with a trio of relievers combining to shut the door on the Mets in the late innings.

The victory marked a promising start to the season for the Giants, who have been one of the most intriguing teams to watch in the NL West. With a talented young core and a deep roster, they have all the ingredients to make a serious push for the playoffs. And with performances like Susac's, it's clear that this team has the potential to make some noise in the coming weeks and months.

As for the Mets, Thursday's loss was a tough pill to swallow, especially coming on the heels of a disappointing road trip. The team will look to bounce back quickly, but it won't be easy. With a tough schedule on the horizon, they'll need to find a way to regroup and refocus if they hope to get back on track. For now, though, the Giants are the ones looking like a team to be reckoned with.

The Giants' win sets the stage for what promises to be an exciting season in the NL West. With the Los Angeles Dodgers, San Diego Padres, and Arizona Diamondbacks all vying for supremacy, the competition is going to be fierce. But with performances like Susac's, it's clear that the Giants are ready to be a part of the conversation. As the season gets underway, one thing is certain: the NL West is going to be a wild ride.
